subject: A large translucent amber-gold cocoon or chrysalis hangs suspended amid dark leaves, with shadowy humanoid figures dimly visible curled inside it
description: A bulbous, translucent sac of amber and golden membrane hangs in the foreground, its surface webbed with darker red veins and cracked patterns. Inside the sac, the dim silhouettes of one or more humanoid bodies can be made out, curled and indistinct, as if cocooned or pupating. The sac is surrounded by broad teal and emerald leaves catching faint light, with tiny floating motes drifting through the air. The background opens onto a shadowed interior space with leaded glass or window framework partially visible behind the foliage, suggesting an overgrown, abandoned structure. The palette is dominated by sickly amber-gold against cool teal-green and deep blue-black shadow.
foreground: Translucent amber cocoon-sac with humanoid silhouettes inside, surrounded by leaves
background: Dark overgrown interior with leaded window framework partially obscured by foliage
emotion: dread, unease, the wrongness of something gestating where it shouldn't be
